Profile

Dylan Lawless, PhD · Zürich

Senior scientist in statistical genomics and translational data science. I build statistical methods, omics workflows, scientific software, and regulated data infrastructure for biomedical research, healthcare, and biotech.

My work connects genomics, multi-omics, clinical data, and real-world evidence through reproducible systems that make complex biomedical data usable, auditable, and reusable.

CV PDF Contact

Profile

I work at the intersection of statistical genomics, translational data science, scientific software, and regulated biomedical infrastructure. I have led analytical delivery across hospital, academic, and product-facing environments, including clinical genomics workflows, multi-omics studies, secure data systems, Bayesian methods, variant interpretation frameworks, and user-facing biomedical software.

My focus is evidence that can be defended: reproducible analyses, explicit uncertainty, traceable data systems, and software that supports decisions beyond the original research setting.

Selected impact

1,000 patients in regulated clinical workflows
5,000 participants in translational studies
100,000+ whole genomes in biobank-scale analyses
100 TB+ secure biomedical data analysed

Role fit

Statistical genomics

Bayesian inference, GWAS, rare variant analysis, uncertainty quantification, population-scale genomics, and reproducible statistical workflows.

Translational data science

Integration of genomics, multi-omics, clinical data, real-world evidence, and biomedical decision systems.

Biomedical software

Scientific packages, structured reporting, full-stack platforms, authenticated systems, and machine-readable outputs.

Regulated infrastructure

Secure computing, traceability, audit-ready workflows, data governance, and hospital-facing analytical systems.

Selected experience

Universitäts-Kinderspital Zürich, senior scientist, 2023 to present

Genomics lead within the Swiss Pediatric Sepsis Study and SwissPedHealth National Data Stream. Built and delivered secure workflows linking whole-genome sequencing, multi-omics, electronic health record data, and high-performance computing across hospital settings.

Focus areas include rare disease diagnostics, paediatric sepsis, clinical genomics infrastructure, secure biomedical data delivery, and translational evidence systems.

EPFL Global Health Institute, senior postdoctoral scientist, 2018 to 2023

Developed statistical genomics and multi-omics workflows across sepsis, tuberculosis, chronic inflammatory disease, and translational cohort studies. Worked across EPFL, ETH Zurich, Swiss TPH, and international partner institutions.

Focus areas included genomic association studies, rare variant analyses, high-dimensional modelling, and integrative biomedical data analysis.

University of Leeds, PhD candidate, 2015 to 2018

Led genomic discovery and functional validation in rare immune disease in a translational hospital setting. Investigated severe clinical cases using genome sequencing, molecular biology, and clinical integration.

Work contributed to novel disease discovery and treatment-related findings in immune dysregulation, autoinflammation, and immunodeficiency.

ACM Global Laboratories, analytical scientist, 2014 to 2015

Worked in a regulated bioanalytical laboratory supporting global clinical trial programmes, biomarker workflows, and quality-controlled assay delivery.

Selected technologies

  • PanelAppRex AI: harmonised disease-gene panels from structured clinical and genetic queries, preprinted in Quant Group et al (2025).

  • VCFheader: parsing VCF headers and generating structured standalone HTML reports, described in Lawless (2026).

  • QuantBayes: standardises how evidence strength is expressed - quantifying genomic variant evidence sufficiency with Bayesian posterior intervals, preprinted in Quant Group et al (2025) .

  • Archipelago: Manhattan plots are for GWAS. Archipelago plots are for complex variant association studies, published in Lawless et al (2026).

View all technologies

Technical interests

Statistics and inference

Bayesian inference, probabilistic modelling, causal inference, DNA, WGS, RNAseq, proteomics, metabolomoics, rare variant association testing, uncertainty quantification, and multi-omics integration.

Scientific software

R, Bash, Python, C, Rust, package development, reproducible workflows, machine-readable reporting, Git, Unix, Linux, command-line tools.

Biomedical platforms

SQL, PostgreSQL, Next.js, React, TypeScript, EHR systems, Supabase, Vercel, authenticated systems, controlled file transfer, and reporting workflows.

Secure infrastructure

High-performance computing, AWS, Azure, Unix systems, Docker, Singularity, Nextflow, Snakemake, encrypted storage, and regulated data environments.

Selected publications

ORCID record: 0000-0001-8496-3725

Application of qualifying variants for genomic analysis. Bioinformatics, 42(2), btaf676, 2026.

Archipelago method for variant set association test statistics. Genetic Epidemiology, 50(1), e70025, 2026.

Quantifying prior probabilities for disease-causing variants reveals the top genetic contributors in inborn errors of immunity. medRxiv, 2025.

PanelAppRex aggregates disease gene panels and facilitates sophisticated search. medRxiv, 2025.

Rare variants in infection response protein pathways associated with sepsis in children. medRxiv, 2025.

A case of adult-onset Still’s disease caused by a novel splicing mutation in TNFAIP3 successfully treated with tocilizumab. Frontiers in Immunology, 9, 2018.

Prevalence and clinical challenges among adult primary immunodeficiency patients with RAG deficiency. Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology, 2018.

Predicting the occurrence of variants in RAG1 and RAG2. Journal of Clinical Immunology, 39(7), 688–701, 2019.

Germline TET2 loss of function causes childhood immunodeficiency and lymphoma. Blood, 136(9), 1055–1066, 2020.

Familial autoinflammation with neutrophilic dermatosis reveals a regulatory mechanism of pyrin activation. Science Translational Medicine, 8(332), 2016.

View all publications

Education and recognition

Education

  • PhD in Medicine, University of Leeds.
  • MSc in Immunology, Trinity College Dublin, first class honours.
  • BSc in Microbiology, University College Cork.

Selected recognition

  • FKZ Children’s Research Centre research prize.
  • Microsoft Azure Research Award, Data Science and ML in Predictive Genomics.
  • Wellcome Genome Campus visitor research grant.
  • University of Leeds 110th Anniversary Postgraduate Research Scholarship.
